summary: Commit 127b381 contains zero test changes — protobuf security bump, a defensive mkdirs() in produceFile, and a smoke-step path correction. No test regressions introduced. All 4 deferred findings from round 1 are acceptable to defer given the stated rationale. No new findings.

commit_verified.regression_risk: none — CoreSettingsModule change adds mkdirs() defensively, does not affect test DataStore which uses TemporaryFolder directly via produceFile = { protoFile }

deferred_findings_disposition.title: concurrent_twoSavesSequential_lastWins misleading name
deferred_findings_disposition.assessment: Accepted. The TDD anchor in task.md (line 45) literally reads 'два последовательных save для одной модели, убедиться что последний выигрывает' — sequential semantics, not concurrent. The test name `concurrent_twoSavesSequential_lastWins` uses 'concurrent' as a taxonomy prefix matching the anchor description label, and 'Sequential' in the body disambiguates. Renaming would diverge from spec. A genuine concurrent variant (parallel coroutine launch) is untested but is scope creep relative to the anchor. Deferred disposition stands.

deferred_findings_disposition.title: ErrorLog.e contract not asserted
deferred_findings_disposition.assessment: Accepted. The implementation at DefaultAppSettingsRepository.kt:25-27 does call errorLog.e(ERROR_COMPONENT, ...) in the observe catch block. Asserting this requires ErrorLog to be injectable as a test double (interface or open class). ErrorLog is in :core-runtime which is out of scope for task 2. MockK is not used in this project. The logging code path IS exercised by ioException_observeDoesNotCrash (the CorruptionException → IOException path is triggered), just not asserted. Deferral to task 3 is appropriate since task 3 extends the ErrorLog whitelist and can introduce open/interface. Deferred disposition stands.

deferred_findings_disposition.title: save/reset IOException paths untested
deferred_findings_disposition.assessment: Accepted. The tech-spec Testing Strategy mandates observe-path IOException coverage only (anchor 6: ioException_observeDoesNotCrash). Save/reset IOException paths require either a writable-to-read-only filesystem trick (unreliable in Robolectric) or a DataStore test double that throws from updateData. Neither is straightforward without MockK. The production code (lines 44-46, 54-56 of DefaultAppSettingsRepository.kt) exists and is correct; the gap is test coverage of those lines, not a logic error. Deferred disposition stands.

deferred_findings_disposition.title: Real ErrorLog side effect in test setUp
deferred_findings_disposition.assessment: Accepted. ErrorLog(context) in setUp is harmless at Robolectric level — android.util.Log calls in Robolectric are no-ops by default and do not write to filesDir. The implicit side effect concern is real but low-impact given Robolectric's sandboxing. Deferred to when ErrorLog is refactored in task 3. Deferred disposition stands.

litmusTest.note: No test code changed in 127b381; litmus re-check not required
